# English Requirements Quality Checklist: i18n / l10n – Energy Burndown Card

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and coverage of English-language requirements, strings, and related documentation for the i18n / l10n feature before and during implementation review.  
**Created**: 2026-03-13  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are English-language requirements clearly defined for all four main user stories (automatic locale, YAML overrides, new language workflow, readable localization keys), and are they traceable to specific IDs (e.g., FR-001–FR-010, SC-001–SC-007)? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories, Spec §FR-001–FR-010, Spec §SC-001–SC-007]
- [ ] CHK002 Are requirements explicitly covering both static labels and dynamic phrases with variable substitution in English (e.g., summaries with `{ percent }`), not just generic “text” references? [Completeness, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements present for English copy related to error/configuration messages, including missing translations, invalid YAML values, and corrupted dictionaries? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK004 Are English wording requirements documented for all card states (normal, loading/initializing, error/blocking), and not only for the happy path display? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008a]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is it clear, in English, which parts of the UI must use localized text versus hard-coded “safety strings” in failure scenarios, and are those distinctions explicitly named? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK006 Are terms like “fallback to English”, “configuration error message”, and “card rendering is blocked” defined precisely enough that reviewers can tell which exact English strings and screens are expected? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK007 Are the expectations for developer-facing English (e.g., inline comments explaining localization keys, console debug logs) spelled out so that reviewers know what “readable code with localization keys” concretely means? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK008 Are English requirements describing locale-reactive behaviour (e.g., re-render on `hass.locale` change) written in a way that clearly states what the user will see in English before and after the change, without ambiguity? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-003a, Spec §SC-007]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Are English descriptions of fallback behaviour (e.g., “fall back to English”) consistent across user stories, functional requirements, and success criteria, with no conflicting statements about when English is used versus when an error is shown? [Consistency, Spec §User Stories 1–3, Spec §FR-007–FR-010, Spec §SC-001–SC-004]
- [ ] CHK010 Are English terms for configuration concepts (e.g., `language`, `number_format`, “override”, “global locale”) used consistently in spec, plan, tasks, and quickstart, so reviewers do not encounter multiple names for the same idea? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004–FR-005, Plan §Summary, Tasks §Phase 4]
- [ ] CHK011 Are English descriptions of edge-case behaviour (e.g., partial `hass.locale`, missing keys, missing dictionary files) aligned between the Edge Cases section, functional requirements, and tasks, with no contradictions? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008–FR-008a, Tasks §Phase 7]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality & Measurability

- [ ] CHK012 Can English-language success criteria for localization (e.g., “100% of visible labels appear in Polish/English”) be objectively verified without guessing which labels are in scope, and is this scope stated explicitly? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria SC-001–SC-003]
- [ ] CHK013 Are there measurable English requirements for the absence of raw user-visible strings in rendering code (e.g., “zero raw string literals”), such that reviewers can treat this as a concrete checklist item? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK014 Do acceptance criteria clearly distinguish between English user-facing copy (UI text) and English developer-facing artifacts (comments, log messages, docs), with separate expectations for each where relevant? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 4, Plan §Summary]

## Scenario & Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK015 Do the English requirements explicitly cover the scenario where HA locale is unsupported (e.g., `de`) and define what the user sees in English (labels, formats) without leaving any behaviour implied? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK016 Are alternate flows involving YAML overrides (e.g., `language: pl`, invalid `number_format`) documented with clear English descriptions of expected text and error states? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-004–FR-005]
- [ ] CHK017 Are recovery/exception flows for missing or corrupt translation files, and for missing keys in both active language and English, written with explicit English messages and behaviour rather than generic “error” wording? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK018 Are requirements describing dynamic English phrases (e.g., summary messages with percentages and periods) covering both primary and edge scenarios (higher/lower/equal, positive/negative values), not just one example? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-006]

## Non-Functional Requirements (English-Focused)

- [ ] CHK019 Are accessibility-related expectations for English text (e.g., clarity of error messages, understandable summary phrases) documented so that reviewers can assess whether the English copy is readable and unambiguous for typical HA users? [Non-Functional, Spec §Success Criteria, Tasks §Phase 7]
- [ ] CHK020 Are there documented expectations that English error/configuration messages must help users self-diagnose i18n issues (e.g., missing keys, invalid overrides) without needing to inspect logs, and are sample phrasings suggested? [Non-Functional, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008a, Tasks §Phase 7]

## Dependencies, Assumptions & Traceability

- [ ] CHK021 Are dependencies on Home Assistant’s own English terminology (e.g., how HA names locales, number formats) identified, and is it clear where the i18n feature should mirror HA phrasing versus define its own? [Dependencies, Spec §Requirements, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK022 Is there a clear mapping between English requirements and corresponding implementation tasks (e.g., which tasks ensure all raw strings are moved to dictionaries, which tasks enforce readable keys), so reviewers can trace coverage? [Traceability, Tasks §Phase 2–Phase 6]
- [ ] CHK023 Are any assumptions about English being the canonical fallback (e.g., always available, always complete) explicitly documented so that future non-English-first contributions can challenge or extend them safely? [Assumptions, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-007–FR-008]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK024 Are potentially ambiguous English phrases like “card rendering is blocked”, “safe empty/loading state”, and “configuration error message” called out and clarified, so that reviewers share the same mental model of what the user actually sees? [Ambiguity, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK025 Have any conflicts between English wording in different docs (e.g., spec vs. quickstart vs. tasks) about how overrides, fallbacks, and error states behave been identified and resolved, or at least noted for follow-up? [Conflict, Spec §User Stories, Plan §Summary, Tasks §Phase 4–Phase 7]

## Notes

- Use this checklist during spec and plan reviews, and again during implementation reviews that involve English copy or localization behaviour.
- When adding or changing requirements, reference CHK IDs in review notes to keep traceability between discussion and requirement quality checks.
